goal: Add per-role model selection support to the Codex adapter and verify it with build/test.
tech_stack: Go, Codex CLI integration, provider/session routing, Go test
summary: Update the Codex provider path so it mirrors the Claude adapterâ€™s role-profile handling: resolve the execution profile for leader/planner/evaluator/worker in the Codex adapter entrypoints, pass that profile or its model into `runStructured`, and append `--model` to `codex exec` only when the resolved model is a Codex/GPT model and not a Claude shorthand such as `opus`, `sonnet`, or `haiku`. Add focused unit coverage for model flag injection and suppression cases, update the relevant status/architecture docs to reflect Codex per-role model selection, and finish with `go build ./...` plus `go test ./...`.
product_scope: Role-profile model propagation for Codex leader/planner/evaluator/worker execution
A helper `isCodexModel(model string) bool` to gate Codex `--model` usage
Provider tests covering `--model` inclusion and exclusion behavior
Documentation updates reflecting Codex per-role model support
Repository verification via `go build ./...` and `go test ./...`
non_goals: Changing Claude adapter behavior beyond preserving compatibility
Adding new provider interface methods or redesigning session routing unless required for the minimal fix
Changing default role profile values in `internal/domain/types.go`
Expanding provider fallback, billing, auth, or retry behavior
Implementing broader model alias normalization outside the Codex gating needed for this task
proposed_steps: Inspect `internal/provider/codex.go`, `internal/provider/claude.go`, and current provider tests to mirror the existing Claude profile flow with the smallest Codex-only change surface.

Modify Codex `RunLeader`, `RunPlanner`, `RunEvaluator`, and `RunWorker` so each resolves the correct role profile from `job.RoleProfiles` and passes it into `runStructured` instead of calling `runStructured` without model context.

Extend Codex `runStructured` to accept the resolved profile or model string, add `isCodexModel(model string) bool`, and append `--model <name>` only when the model is non-empty and allowed for Codex; suppress the flag for Claude shorthand models such as `opus`, `sonnet`, and `haiku`.
Keep the implementation cross-platform and localized to provider code; avoid mixing harness semantics or changing unrelated orchestration behavior.

Add or update unit tests in `internal/provider/provider_test.go` to assert that Codex receives `--model` for GPT-style models, omits it for empty model strings, and omits it for Claude shorthand role models routed through the Codex adapter.
Update the relevant docs, at minimum the implementation status entry for the Codex adapter, so the documented behavior matches the new per-role model-selection support.
Run `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` and capture the pass/fail result as final verification evidence.
success_signals: A Codex adapter test inspects constructed args and finds `--model` only for GPT-style inputs.
A Codex adapter test using role profiles like planner=`opus` or executor=`sonnet` passes without expecting a `--model` flag.
No provider-interface regression appears in existing planner/evaluator/worker tests.
The implementation status doc no longer understates Codex model-selection capability.
Both repository-wide verification commands complete successfully.

verification_contract.goal: Verify that Codex provider executions honor role-profile model selection without passing Claude shorthand models to `codex exec`, and that the repo still builds and tests cleanly.
scope: Provider unit tests for model flag behavior
Documentation alignment for Codex adapter capabilities
required_checks: Confirm Codex leader/planner/evaluator/worker paths use the resolved role profile model.
Confirm `isCodexModel` accepts empty and GPT-style model names and rejects Claude shorthand names for Codex flag injection.
Confirm test coverage asserts both `--model` inclusion and suppression cases.
Confirm build and test commands pass from the workspace root.
disallowed_actions: Do not change evaluator gate semantics.
Do not modify Claude adapter behavior unless required for compatibility.
Do not redesign provider/session interfaces beyond the minimal wiring needed for this feature.
Do not skip `go build ./...` or `go test ./...` verification.
verification_contract.notes: Prefer the minimal implementation pattern already used by `internal/provider/claude.go`: keep role-profile resolution close to the adapter entrypoints unless a broader provider API change is strictly necessary.
